svg.text: Background = the model's forecast move per round ρ·σ; each dot =
the run's observed average move per round — same units
one dot per run · 67 modelable runs from experiments/spread_util_unified.json (round-1 record per run)
Dot = (final value + drift − round-1 value) ÷ round count (4 / 8 / 2 rounds); background = ρ·σ. Both are
value change per round — a dot matching the gradient behind it is the forecast holding.
ρσ = 0
round-1 agreement ρ = correlation of judge scores with candidate value scores (−1 disagree → +1 lockstep)
round-1 spread σ = within-prompt SD of value scores
One scale, one unit: move per round
Dot fill = the run's OBSERVED mean
per-round move = (endpoint − round-1
value) ÷ round count
Background = the model's FORECAST
one-round move = agreement ρ × spread σ
+0.25 / rnd value climbs
0 no move
−0.25 / rnd value falls
same red/gray/blue scale for both,
now both in value-change-per-round.
Does dot color match the background?
35 of the 44 runs that averaged ≥ 0.04 per round sit on a
background of the matching color — the sign of the mean
per-round move equals the sign of the forecast ρ·σ.
Caveat: the 0/1 value walls dilute strong runs. Averaging over the whole run flattens a run that hits the 0 or 1 wall early — its late rounds add
~zero move — so a wall-hitting run reads paler here than its true early force. The walls are the reason, not a weak force.
